chapter_context.summary: A warning against legalism and 'confidence in the flesh.' Paul describes his own transition from a Pharisee to a follower of Christ, counting all things as loss to know Him.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Goal of Knowing Christ (3:1-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Philippians.
This section focuses on Pressing Toward the Mark (3:12-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Philippians.

1.commentary:
### Tyndale Study Notes
**Section Context: The Goal of Knowing Christ**
This verse in Philippians 3 reflects Paul's deep joy and his desire for the believers to be united and matured in their walk with Christ.
1.full_commentary:
### Matthew Henry Commentary
Matthew Henry highlights the 'peace of God' and the 'joy of the Lord' as portrayed in this encouraging passage.

### Jamieson-Fausset-Brown
JFB analysis explores the personal bond between Paul and the Philippians, as well as the profound Christology of Chapter 2.

key_themes.title: Joy in Christ
key_themes.description: Rejoicing in the Lord regardless of external circumstances.

key_themes.title: The Mind of Christ
key_themes.description: Humility and servanthood as modeled by Jesus.

key_themes.title: Contentment
key_themes.description: Finding satisfaction in God's provision in every situation.

devotional.question: What does it mean for you to have 'the mind of Christ' in your current relationships?

cultural_historical.title: The Roman Colony
cultural_historical.text: Philippi was a Roman colony, and its citizens were proud of their Roman status. Paul reminds them that their true 'citizenship is in heaven'.
